### Deployment Output Example

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/2.deploy/20.providers/zephyr.md

Example output displayed in the terminal after a successful Zephyr deployment.

```txt
◐ Building [Nitro] (preset: zephyr, compatibility: YYYY-MM-DD)
...
ZEPHYR   Uploaded local snapshot in 110ms
ZEPHYR   Deployed to Zephyr's edge in 700ms.
ZEPHYR
ZEPHYR   https://my-app.zephyrcloud.app
```

--------------------------------

### GET /api/hello

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/4.examples/api-routes.md

A basic API route example that returns a static string.

```APIDOC
## GET /api/hello

### Description
Returns a simple greeting message.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/api/hello

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **body** (string) - The message "Nitro is amazing!"

### Response Example
"Nitro is amazing!"
```

--------------------------------

### Example Vite and Nitro Configuration

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/2.quick-start.md

A combined example showing Vite configuration with the Nitro plugin and Nitro's server directory configuration.

```typescript
import { defineConfig } from "vite";
import { nitro } from "nitro/vite";

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [nitro()]
});
```

```typescript
import { defineConfig } from "nitro";

export default defineConfig({
  serverDir: "./server",
});
```

```typescript
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";

export default defineHandler(() => {
  return { message: "Hello Nitro!" };
});
```

--------------------------------

### KV Storage Methods Examples

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/8.storage.md

Illustrates common KV storage operations including getting keys, checking existence, removing items, and retrieving raw data or metadata. Shorthand aliases like `get`, `set`, `has`, `del` are also available.

```typescript
import { useStorage } from "nitro/storage";

// Get all keys under a prefix
const keys = await useStorage("test").getKeys();

// Check if a key exists
const exists = await useStorage().hasItem("test:foo");

// Remove a key
await useStorage().removeItem("test:foo");

// Get raw binary data
const raw = await useStorage().getItemRaw("assets/server:image.png");

// Get metadata (type, etag, mtime, etc.)
const meta = await useStorage("assets/server").getMeta("file.txt");
```

--------------------------------

### Usage Example

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/50.database.md

Example of how to use the `useDatabase` hook to interact with the database in a handler.

```APIDOC
## Usage Example

```ts [server.ts]
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";
import { useDatabase } from "nitro/database";

export default defineHandler(async () => {
  const db = useDatabase();

  // Create users table
  await db.sql`DROP TABLE IF EXISTS users`;
  await db.sql`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users ("id" TEXT PRIMARY KEY, "firstName" TEXT, "lastName" TEXT, "email" TEXT)`;

  // Add a new user
  const userId = String(Math.round(Math.random() * 10_000));
  await db.sql`INSERT INTO users VALUES (${userId}, 'John', 'Doe', '')`;

  // Query for users
  const { rows } = await db.sql`SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${userId}`;

  return {
    rows,
  };
});
```
```

--------------------------------

### GET /api/hello/:name

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/4.examples/api-routes.md

A dynamic route example that captures a URL parameter.

```APIDOC
## GET /api/hello/:name

### Description
Returns a greeting using the dynamic name parameter provided in the URL.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/api/hello/:name

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **name** (string) - Required - The dynamic segment representing the name.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **body** (string) - A greeting string including the name parameter.

### Response Example
"Hello (param: world)!"
```

--------------------------------

### Hono Framework Integration

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/6.server-entry.md

Integrates the Hono framework as a server entry. This example sets up a simple GET route for the root path.

```typescript
import { Hono } from "hono";

const app = new Hono();

app.get("/", (c) => c.text("🔥 Hello from Hono!"));

export default app;
```

--------------------------------

### Define Start Script in package.json

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/2.deploy/20.providers/digitalocean.md

Add a start script to package.json that points to the Nitro output server entry point, allowing DigitalOcean to execute the application.

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "start": "node .output/server/index.mjs"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create server entry point in server.ts

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/4.examples/vite-ssr-tss-react.md

Initializes the TanStack Start server entry point for handling requests.

```ts
import handler, { createServerEntry } from "@tanstack/react-start/server-entry";

export default createServerEntry({
  fetch(request) {
    return handler.fetch(request);
  },
});
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Development Server Proxy

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/3.config/0.index.md

Set up proxy configurations for the development server. This example shows proxying '/proxy/test' to a local server and '/proxy/example' to an external URL with origin change.

```javascript
{
  devProxy: {
    '/proxy/test': 'http://localhost:3001',
    '/proxy/example': { target: 'https://example.com', changeOrigin: true }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### CI/CD Deployment Workflow

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/2.deploy/20.providers/zephyr.md

Example GitHub Actions workflow for deploying to Zephyr using a secret API token.

```yaml
name: Deploy with Zephyr

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      ZE_SECRET_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.ZEPHYR_AUTH_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
```

--------------------------------

### Runtime Route Rules Configuration

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/5.routing.md

Example of how to define route rules using `runtimeConfig` for environment variable overrides.

```APIDOC
## Runtime Route Rules

Route rules can be configured via `runtimeConfig` to allow overrides using environment variables without requiring a rebuild.

### Example (`nitro.config.ts`)

```ts
import { defineConfig } from "nitro";

export default defineConfig({
  runtimeConfig: {
    nitro: {
      routeRules: {
        '/api/**': { headers: { 'x-env': 'production' } },
      },
    },
  },
});
```
```

--------------------------------

### Elysia Framework Integration

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/6.server-entry.md

Integrates the Elysia framework as a server entry. This example sets up a simple GET route for the root path.

```typescript
import { Elysia } from "elysia";

const app = new Elysia();

app.get("/", () => "🦊 Hello from Elysia!");

export default app.compile();
```

--------------------------------

### Define project dependencies in package.json

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/4.examples/vite-ssr-tss-react.md

Lists the required dependencies and scripts for a TanStack Start project using Nitro.

```json
{
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "build": "vite build",
    "dev": "vite dev",
    "start": "node .output/server/index.mjs"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@tanstack/react-router": "^1.168.8",
    "@tanstack/react-router-devtools": "^1.166.11",
    "@tanstack/react-start": "^1.167.13",
    "nitro": "latest",
    "react": "^19.2.4",
    "react-dom": "^19.2.4",
    "tailwind-merge": "^3.5.0",
    "zod": "^4.3.6"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@tailwindcss/vite": "^4.2.2",
    "@types/node": "latest",
    "@types/react": "^19.2.14",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.2.3",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^6.0.1",
    "tailwindcss": "^4.2.2",
    "typescript": "^6.0.2",
    "vite": "latest",
    "vite-tsconfig-paths": "^6.1.1"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### API Route Example

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/4.renderer.md

An example of a simple API route handler that returns a JSON object. This route will be handled before the renderer.

```typescript
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";

export default defineHandler((event) => {
  return { hello: "API" };
});
```

--------------------------------

### H3 Framework Integration

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/6.server-entry.md

Integrates the H3 framework as a server entry. This example sets up a simple GET route for the root path.

```typescript
import { H3 } from "h3";

const app = new H3()

app.get("/", () => "⚡️ Hello from H3!");

export default app;
```

--------------------------------

### Fastify Framework Integration (Node.js)

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/6.server-entry.md

Integrates the Fastify framework using a `server.node.ts` file for Node.js compatibility. This example sets up a simple GET route for the root path.

```typescript
import Fastify from "fastify";

const app = Fastify();

app.get("/", () => "Hello, Fastify with Nitro!");

await app.ready();

export default app.routing;
```

--------------------------------

### Custom amplify.yml for Single Project

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/2.deploy/20.providers/aws-amplify.md

A sample `amplify.yml` file for a single-project setup. It specifies build commands, including Node.js version selection and dependency installation, and defines the output directory for build artifacts.

```yaml
version: 1
frontend:
  phases:
    preBuild:
      commands:
        - nvm use 24 && node --version
        - corepack enable && npx --yes nypm install
    build:
      commands:
        - pnpm build
  artifacts:
    baseDirectory: .amplify-hosting
    files:
      - "**/*"
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Vite in vite.config.mjs

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/4.examples/vite-ssr-tss-react.md

Sets up the Vite plugins for TanStack Start, Nitro, React, and Tailwind CSS.

```js
import { defineConfig } from "vite";
import { nitro } from "nitro/vite";
import { tanstackStart } from "@tanstack/react-start/plugin/vite";
import viteReact from "@vitejs/plugin-react";
import viteTsConfigPaths from "vite-tsconfig-paths";
import tailwindcss from "@tailwindcss/vite";

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [
    viteTsConfigPaths({ projects: ["./tsconfig.json"] }),
    tanstackStart(),
    viteReact(),
    tailwindcss(),
    nitro(),
  ],
  environments: {
    ssr: { build: { rollupOptions: { input: "./server.ts" } } },
  },
});
```

--------------------------------

### Create Home Page Route

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/examples/vite-ssr-tss-react/README.md

This React component snippet defines a simple home page route using TanStack Start's file-based routing. It renders a welcome message and a link to an API endpoint.

```tsx
import { createFileRoute } from "@tanstack/react-router";

export const Route = createFileRoute("/")({ component: Home });

function Home() {
  return (
    <div className="p-2">
      <h3>Welcome Home!</h3>
      <a href="/api/test">/api/test</a>
    </div>
  );
}
```

--------------------------------

### WebSocket Client Example (HTML/JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/4.examples/websocket.md

Provides an example of a client-side HTML page with embedded JavaScript to connect to a WebSocket server. It uses `petite-vue` for reactivity and `shiki` for code highlighting of JSON messages. This example demonstrates sending messages, receiving them, and displaying them in a chat-like interface.

```html
<html lang="en" data-theme="dark">
  <head>
    <title>CrossWS Test Page</title>
    <script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script>
    <style>
      body {
        background-color: #1a1a1a;
      }
    </style>
    <script type="module">
      import { createApp, reactive, nextTick } from "https://esm.sh/petite-vue@0.4.1";

      let ws;

      const store = reactive({
        message: "",
        messages: [],
      });

      const scroll = () => {
        nextTick(() => {
          const el = document.querySelector("#messages");
          el.scrollTop = el.scrollHeight;
          el.scrollTo({
            top: el.scrollHeight,
            behavior: "smooth",
          });
        });
      };

      const format = async () => {
        for (const message of store.messages) {
          if (!message._fmt && message.text.startsWith("{")) {
            message._fmt = true;
            const { codeToHtml } = await import("https://esm.sh/shiki@1.0.0");
            const str = JSON.stringify(JSON.parse(message.text), null, 2);
            message.formattedText = await codeToHtml(str, {
              lang: "json",
              theme: "dark-plus",
            });
          }
        }
      };

      const log = (user, ...args) => {
        console.log("[ws]", user, ...args);
        store.messages.push({
          text: args.join(" "),
          formattedText: "",
          user: user,
          date: new Date().toLocaleString(),
        });
        scroll();
        format();
      };

      const connect = async () => {
        const isSecure = location.protocol === "https:";
        const url = (isSecure ? "wss://" : "ws://") + location.host + "/_ws";
        if (ws) {
          log("ws", "Closing previous connection before reconnecting...");
          ws.close();
          clear();
        }

        log("ws", "Connecting to", url, "...");
        ws = new WebSocket(url);

        ws.addEventListener("message", async (event) => {
          let data = typeof event.data === "string" ? event.data : await event.data.text();
          const { user = "system", message = "" } = data.startsWith("{")
            ? JSON.parse(data)
            : { message: data };
          log(user, typeof message === "string" ? message : JSON.stringify(message));
        });

        await new Promise((resolve) => ws.addEventListener("open", resolve));
        log("ws", "Connected!");
      };

      const clear = () => {
        store.messages.splice(0, store.messages.length);
        log("system", "previous messages cleared");
      };

      const send = () => {
        console.log("sending message...");
        if (store.message) {
          ws.send(store.message);
        }
        store.message = "";
      };

      const ping = () => {
        log("ws", "Sending ping");
        ws.send("ping");
      };

      createApp({
        store,
        send,
        ping,
        clear,
        connect,
        rand: Math.random(),
      }).mount();

      await connect();
    </script>
  </head>
  <body class="h-screen flex flex-col justify-between">
    <main v-scope="{}">
      <!-- Messages -->
      <div id="messages" class="flex-grow flex flex-col justify-end px-4 py-8">
        <div class="flex items-center mb-4" v-for="message in store.messages">
          <div class="flex flex-col">
            <p class="text-gray-500 mb-1 text-xs ml-10">{{ message.user }}</p>
            <div class="flex items-center">
              <img
                :src="'https://www.gravatar.com/avatar/' + encodeURIComponent(message.user + rand) + '?s=512&d=monsterid'"
                alt="Avatar"
                class="w-8 h-8 rounded-full"
              />
              <div class="ml-2 bg-gray-800 rounded-lg p-2">
                <p
                  v-if="message.formattedText"
                  class="overflow-x-scroll"
                  v-html="message.formattedText"
                ></p>
                <p v-else class="text-white">{{ message.text }}</p>
              </div>
            </div>
            <p class="text-gray-500 mt-1 text-xs ml-10">{{ message.date }}</p>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>

      <!-- Chatbox -->
      <div class="bg-gray-800 px-4 py-2 flex items-center justify-between fixed bottom-0 w-full">
        <div class="w-full min-w-6">
          <input
            type="text"
            placeholder="Type your message..."
            class="w-full rounded-l-lg px-4 py-2 bg-gray-700 text-white focus:outline-none focus:ring focus:border-blue-300"
            @keydown.enter="send"
            v-model="store.message"
          />
        </div>
        <div class="flex">

```

--------------------------------

### Dynamic HTML Template with Nitro

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/4.renderer.md

Example of using template syntax and server-side scripts within an HTML file.

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8" />
    <title>Dynamic template</title>
  </head>
  <body>
    <h1>Hello {{ $REQUEST.url }}</h1>
    <p>Welcome, <?= $COOKIES["user"] || "Guest" ?>!</p>
    <script server>
      setCookie("visited", "true", { maxAge: 3600 });
    </script>
  </body>
</html>
```

--------------------------------

### Server Entry Example with Custom Logic

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/6.server-entry.md

This snippet demonstrates a `server.ts` file that handles a `/health` route and allows other requests to proceed. It adds custom headers to all requests.

```typescript
export default {
  async fetch(req: Request) {
    const url = new URL(req.url);

    // Handle specific routes
    if (url.pathname === "/health") {
      return new Response("OK", {
        status: 200,
        headers: { "content-type": "text/plain" }
      });
    }

    // Add custom headers to all requests
    // Return nothing to continue to the next handler
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Accessing Server Assets

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/8.storage.md

Provides an example of accessing server assets, which are automatically mounted at the `/assets` base path. This mount point offers read-only access to bundled server assets.

```typescript
import { useStorage } from "nitro/storage";

// Access server assets via the /assets mount
const content = await useStorage("assets/server").getItem("my-file.txt");
```

--------------------------------

### KV Storage Usage

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/8.storage.md

Demonstrates basic usage of the `useStorage()` utility for setting and getting items, including specifying a base prefix and using generics for type safety.

```APIDOC
## KV Storage Usage

### Description
Basic usage of the `useStorage()` utility for setting and getting items, including specifying a base prefix and using generics for type safety.

### Method
`useStorage(base?)`

### Endpoint
N/A (Utility function)

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **base** (string) - Optional - The base prefix for the storage.

### Request Example
```ts
import { useStorage } from "nitro/storage";

// Default storage (in-memory)
await useStorage().setItem("test:foo", { hello: "world" });
const value = await useStorage().getItem("test:foo");

// You can specify a base prefix with useStorage(base)
const testStorage = useStorage("test");
await testStorage.setItem("foo", { hello: "world" });
await testStorage.getItem("foo"); // { hello: "world" }

// You can use generics to type the return value
await useStorage<{ hello: string }>("test").getItem("foo");
await useStorage("test").getItem<{ hello: string }>("foo");
```

### Response
N/A (This is a utility function, not an API endpoint with direct request/response)

```

--------------------------------

### Custom HTML Template File

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/4.renderer.md

An example of a custom HTML file (`app.html`) used as the renderer template. It includes a placeholder for the application root and script.

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8" />
    <title>Custom Template</title>
  </head>
  <body>
    <div id="root">Loading...</div>
    <script type="module" src="/src/main.js"></script>
  </body>
</html>
```

--------------------------------

### Access Server Assets via Storage Layer

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/50.assets.md

Example Nitro handler demonstrating how to retrieve server assets (e.g., data.json) from the 'assets:server' mount point using the storage layer.

```javascript
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";

export default defineHandler(async () => {
  const data = await useStorage("assets:server").get("data.json");

  return data;
});
```

--------------------------------

### Example Cache Key for getAccessToken

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/7.cache.md

Illustrates a concrete cache key generated for a specific cached function, showing how the defined options and `getKey` contribute to the final key.

```typescript
import { defineCachedFunction } from "nitro/cache";

const getAccessToken = defineCachedFunction(() => {
  return String(Date.now())
}, {
  maxAge: 10,
  name: "getAccessToken",
  getKey: () => "default"
});
```

```plaintext
cache:nitro/functions:getAccessToken:default.json
```

--------------------------------

### Production Public Assets Manifest

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/50.assets.md

Example of a manifest file created during build for production public assets. It contains metadata like type, ETag, modification time, and size for each asset.

```json
{
  "/image.png": {
    "type": "image/png",
    "etag": "\"4a0c-6utWq0Kbk5OqDmksYCa9XV8irnM\"",
    "mtime": "2023-03-04T21:39:45.086Z",
    "size": 18956
  },
  "/robots.txt": {
    "type": "text/plain; charset=utf-8",
    "etag": "\"8-hMqyDrA8fJ0R904zgEPs3L55Jls\"",
    "mtime": "2023-03-04T21:39:45.086Z",
    "size": 8
  },
  "/video.mp4": {
    "type": "video/mp4",
    "etag": "\"9b943-4UwfQXKUjPCesGPr6J5j7GzNYGU\"",
    "mtime": "2023-03-04T21:39:45.085Z",
    "size": 637251
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### POST /api/highlight

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/examples/shiki/README.md

This endpoint accepts raw code in the request body and returns an HTML string containing the syntax-highlighted version of the code using the Vitesse Dark theme.

```APIDOC
## POST /api/highlight

### Description
Accepts raw code as a string in the request body and returns the corresponding syntax-highlighted HTML.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/api/highlight

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **code** (string) - Required - The raw source code to be highlighted.

### Request Example
```text
console.log("Hello World");
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **html** (string) - The rendered HTML string with syntax highlighting classes.

#### Response Example
```html
<pre class="shiki vitesse-dark"><code><span class="line">console.log("Hello World");</span></code></pre>
```
```

--------------------------------

### React Main Entry Point

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/4.examples/vite-ssr-tsr-react.md

Sets up the React application with TanStack Router, creating and rendering the router instance.

```typescript
import { StrictMode } from "react";
import ReactDOM from "react-dom/client";
import { RouterProvider, createRouter } from "@tanstack/react-router";

// Import the generated route tree
import { routeTree } from "./routeTree.gen.ts";

// Create a new router instance
const router = createRouter({ routeTree });

// Register the router instance for type safety
declare module "@tanstack/react-router" {
  interface Register {
    router: typeof router;
  }
}

// Render the app
const rootElement = document.querySelector("#root")!;
if (!rootElement.innerHTML) {
  const root = ReactDOM.createRoot(rootElement);
  root.render(
    <StrictMode>
      <RouterProvider router={router} />
    </StrictMode>
  );
}
```

--------------------------------

### Server Entry Point (index.ts)

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/4.examples/vite-ssr-tsr-react.md

Set up the server entry point to handle incoming requests, render the React app using TanStack Router, and return the HTML.

```typescript
import express from "express";
import { renderToString } from "react-dom/server";
import { createRouter } from "@tanstack/react-router-server";
import { routeTree } from "./routeTree.gen";

const app = express();

app.get("*", async (req, res) => {
  const router = createRouter({
    routeTree,
    context: { /* your context */ },
    location: req.url,
  });

  const appHtml = renderToString(router.render());

  res.status(200).send(`
    <!DOCTYPE html>
    <html>
      <head>
        <title>Vite SSR App</title>
      </head>
      <body>
        <div id="root">${appHtml}</div>
        <script type="module" src="/src/main.tsx"></script>
      </body>
    </html>
  `);
});

app.listen(3000, () => {
  console.log("Server listening on http://localhost:3000");
});

```

--------------------------------

### Build and run Nitro with Deno

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/2.deploy/10.runtimes/deno.md

Use the deno_server preset to build the application and execute it with the required Deno permissions.

```bash
# Build with the deno NITRO preset
NITRO_PRESET=deno_server npm run build

# Start production server
deno run --unstable --allow-net --allow-read --allow-env .output/server/index.ts
```

--------------------------------

### Implement Server Entry

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/4.examples/fastify.md

The server entry point for Node.js. Ensure app.ready() is called and app.routing is exported.

```ts
import Fastify from "fastify";

const app = Fastify();

app.get("/", () => "Hello, Fastify with Nitro!");

await app.ready();

export default app.routing;
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Nitro Modules and Hooks

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/3.config/0.index.md

Include Nitro modules by providing paths or module objects with setup functions. This example shows how to register a module and hook into the 'compiled' event.

```typescript
export default defineConfig({
  modules: [
    "./modules/my-module.ts",
    (nitro) => {
      nitro.hooks.hook("compiled", () => { /* ... */ });
    },
  ],
});
```

--------------------------------

### KV Storage Available Methods

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/8.storage.md

Lists and describes the available methods for interacting with the storage instance obtained via `useStorage()`, including shorthand aliases.

```APIDOC
## KV Storage Available Methods

### Description
Lists and describes the available methods for interacting with the storage instance obtained via `useStorage()`, including shorthand aliases.

### Methods

| Method | Description |
|---|---|
| `getItem(key)` | Get the value of a key. Returns `null` if the key does not exist. |
| `getItems(items)` | Get multiple items at once. Accepts an array of keys or `{ key, options }` objects. |
| `getItemRaw(key)` | Get the raw value of a key without parsing. Useful for binary data. |
| `setItem(key, value)` | Set the value of a key. |
| `setItems(items)` | Set multiple items at once. Accepts an array of `{ key, value }` objects. |
| `setItemRaw(key, value)` | Set the raw value of a key without serialization. |
| `hasItem(key)` | Check if a key exists. Returns a boolean. |
| `removeItem(key)` | Remove a key from storage. |
| `getKeys(base?)` | Get all keys, optionally filtered by a base prefix. |
| `clear(base?)` | Clear all keys, optionally filtered by a base prefix. |
| `getMeta(key)` | Get metadata for a key (e.g., `mtime`, `atime`, `ttl`). |
| `setMeta(key, meta)` | Set metadata for a key. |
| `removeMeta(key)` | Remove metadata for a key. |
| `mount(base, driver)` | Dynamically mount a storage driver at a base path. |
| `unmount(base)` | Unmount a storage driver from a base path. |
| `watch(callback)` | Watch for changes. Callback receives `(event, key)` where event is `"update"` or `"remove"`. |
| `unwatch()` | Stop watching for changes. |

Shorthand aliases are also available: `get`, `set`, `has`, `del`, `remove`, `keys`.

### Request Example
```ts
import { useStorage } from "nitro/storage";

// Get all keys under a prefix
const keys = await useStorage("test").getKeys();

// Check if a key exists
const exists = await useStorage().hasItem("test:foo");

// Remove a key
await useStorage().removeItem("test:foo");

// Get raw binary data
const raw = await useStorage().getItemRaw("assets/server:image.png");

// Get metadata (type, etag, mtime, etc.)
const meta = await useStorage("assets/server").getMeta("file.txt");
```

### Response
N/A (This section describes methods, not direct API responses)

```

--------------------------------

### Create Server Entry for SSR

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/4.examples/vite-ssr-vue-router.md

Sets up the server entry point for Server-Side Rendering (SSR). It creates a Vue app, configures the router with memory history, dynamically loads route assets, manages head elements using `unhead`, and renders the app to a string.

```typescript
import { createSSRApp } from "vue";
import { renderToString } from "vue/server-renderer";
import { RouterView, createMemoryHistory, createRouter } from "vue-router";
import { createHead, transformHtmlTemplate } from "unhead/server";

import { routes } from "./routes.ts";

import clientAssets from "./entry-client.ts?assets=client";

async function handler(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const app = createSSRApp(RouterView);
  const router = createRouter({ history: createMemoryHistory(), routes });
  app.use(router);

  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const href = url.href.slice(url.origin.length);

  await router.push(href);
  await router.isReady();

  const assets = clientAssets.merge(
    ...(await Promise.all(
      router.currentRoute.value.matched
        .map((to) => to.meta.assets)
        .filter(Boolean)
        .map((fn) => (fn as any)().then((m: any) => m.default))
    ))
  );

  const head = createHead();

  head.push({
    link: [
      ...assets.css.map((attrs: any) => ({ rel: "stylesheet", ...attrs })),
      ...assets.js.map((attrs: any) => ({ rel: "modulepreload", ...attrs }))
    ],
    script: [{ type: "module", src: clientAssets.entry }],
  });

  const renderedApp = await renderToString(app);

  const html = await transformHtmlTemplate(head, htmlTemplate(renderedApp));

  return new Response(html, {
    headers: { "Content-Type": "text/html;charset=utf-8" },
  });
}

function htmlTemplate(body: string): string {
  return /* html */ `<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
  <title>Vue Router Custom Framework</title>
</head>
<body>
  <div id="root">${body}</div>
</body>
</html>`;
}

export default {
  fetch: handler,
};
```

--------------------------------

### Cached Function Example (JSON Output)

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/7.cache.md

Example of cached data structure for a function. The cache stores expiration time, value, modification time, and integrity.

```json
{
"expires":1677851092249,
"value":43991,
"mtime":1677847492540,
"integrity":"ZUHcsxCWEH"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Client and Server Entry Points

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/4.examples/vite-ssr-vue-router.md

Entry points for client-side hydration and server-side rendering logic.

```typescript
import { createSSRApp } from "vue";
import { RouterView, createRouter, createWebHistory } from "vue-router";
import { routes } from "./routes.ts";

async function main() {
  const app = createSSRApp(RouterView);
  const router = createRouter({ history: createWebHistory(), routes });
  app.use(router);

  await router.isReady();
  app.mount("#root");
}

// eslint-disable-next-line unicorn/prefer-top-level-await
main();
```

```typescript
import { createSSRApp } from "vue";
import { renderToString } from "vue/server-renderer";
import { RouterView, createMemoryHistory, createRouter } from "vue-router";
import { createHead, transformHtmlTemplate } from "unhead/server";

import { routes } from "./routes.ts";

import clientAssets from "./entry-client.ts?assets=client";

async function handler(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const app = createSSRApp(RouterView);
  const router = createRouter({ history: createMemoryHistory(), routes });
  app.use(router);

  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const href = url.href.slice(url.origin.length);

  await router.push(href);
  await router.isReady();

  const assets = clientAssets.merge(
    ...(await Promise.all(
      router.currentRoute.value.matched
        .map((to) => to.meta.assets)
        .filter(Boolean)
        .map((fn) => (fn as any)().then((m: any) => m.default))
    ))
  );

  const head = createHead();

  head.push({
    link: [
      ...assets.css.map((attrs: any) => ({ rel: "stylesheet", ...attrs })),
      ...assets.js.map((attrs: any) => ({ rel: "modulepreload", ...attrs })),
    ],
    script: [{ type: "module", src: clientAssets.entry }],
  });

  const renderedApp = await renderToString(app);

  const html = await transformHtmlTemplate(head, htmlTemplate(renderedApp));

  return new Response(html, {
    headers: { "Content-Type": "text/html;charset=utf-8" },
  });
}

function htmlTemplate(body: string): string {
  return /* html */ `<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
  <title>Vue Router Custom Framework</title>
</head>
<body>
  <div id="root">${body}</div>
</body>
</html>`;
}

export default {
  fetch: handler,
};
```

--------------------------------

### Define Nitro Configuration

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/50.configuration.md

Basic setup for a Nitro configuration file.

```ts
import { defineConfig } from "nitro";

export default defineConfig({
  // Nitro options
})
```

--------------------------------

### Nitro Project Setup with npm

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/4.examples/custom-error-handler.md

This `package.json` file outlines the basic setup for a Nitro project using npm. It defines scripts for development (`nitro dev`) and building (`nitro build`), and specifies `nitro` as a development dependency. This is essential for running and building your Nitro application.

```json
{
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "nitro dev",
    "build": "nitro build"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "nitro": "latest"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Browser Entry Point Logic

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/4.examples/vite-rsc.md

Initializes the client-side application by deserializing the RSC stream, setting up state management for RSC payloads, and handling client-side navigation and server function calls. It also includes logic for server-side hot module replacement (HMR).

```tsx
import {
  createFromReadableStream,
  createFromFetch,
  setServerCallback,
  createTemporaryReferenceSet,
  encodeReply,
} from "@vitejs/plugin-rsc/browser";
import React from "react";
import { createRoot, hydrateRoot } from "react-dom/client";
import { rscStream } from "rsc-html-stream/client";
import { GlobalErrorBoundary } from "./error-boundary";
import type { RscPayload } from "./entry.rsc";
import { createRscRenderRequest } from "./request";

async function main() {
  // Stash `setPayload` function to trigger re-rendering
  // from outside of `BrowserRoot` component (e.g. server function call, navigation, hmr)
  let setPayload: (v: RscPayload) => void;

  // Deserialize RSC stream back to React VDOM for CSR
  const initialPayload = await createFromReadableStream<RscPayload>(
    // Initial RSC stream is injected in SSR stream as <script>...FLIGHT_DATA...</script>
    rscStream
  );

  // Browser root component to (re-)render RSC payload as state
  function BrowserRoot() {
    const [payload, setPayload_] = React.useState(initialPayload);

    React.useEffect(() => {
      setPayload = (v) => React.startTransition(() => setPayload_(v));
    }, [setPayload_]);

    // Re-fetch/render on client side navigation
    React.useEffect(() => {
      return listenNavigation(() => fetchRscPayload());
    }, []);

    return payload.root;
  }

  // Re-fetch RSC and trigger re-rendering
  async function fetchRscPayload() {
    const renderRequest = createRscRenderRequest(globalThis.location.href);
    const payload = await createFromFetch<RscPayload>(fetch(renderRequest));
    setPayload(payload);
  }

  // Register a handler which will be internally called by React
  // on server function request after hydration.
  setServerCallback(async (id, args) => {
    const temporaryReferences = createTemporaryReferenceSet();
    const renderRequest = createRscRenderRequest(globalThis.location.href, {
      id,
      body: await encodeReply(args, { temporaryReferences }),
    });
    const payload = await createFromFetch<RscPayload>(fetch(renderRequest), {
      temporaryReferences,
    });
    setPayload(payload);
    const { ok, data } = payload.returnValue!;
    if (!ok) throw data;
    return data;
  });

  // Hydration
  const browserRoot = (
    <React.StrictMode>
      <GlobalErrorBoundary>
        <BrowserRoot />
      </GlobalErrorBoundary>
    </React.StrictMode>
  );
  if ("__NO_HYDRATE" in globalThis) {
    createRoot(document).render(browserRoot);
  } else {
    hydrateRoot(document, browserRoot, {
      formState: initialPayload.formState,
    });
  }

  // Implement server HMR by triggering re-fetch/render of RSC upon server code change
  if (import.meta.hot) {
    import.meta.hot.on("rsc:update", () => {
      fetchRscPayload();
    });
  }
}

// A little helper to setup events interception for client side navigation
function listenNavigation(onNavigation: () => void) {
  globalThis.addEventListener("popstate", onNavigation);

  const oldPushState = globalThis.history.pushState;
  globalThis.history.pushState = function (...args) {
    const res = oldPushState.apply(this, args);
    onNavigation();
    return res;
  };

  const oldReplaceState = globalThis.history.replaceState;
  globalThis.history.replaceState = function (...args) {
    const res = oldReplaceState.apply(this, args);
    onNavigation();
    return res;
  };

  function onClick(e: MouseEvent) {

```

--------------------------------

### Client Entry Point (main.tsx)

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/4.examples/vite-ssr-tsr-react.md

Initialize TanStack Router on the client-side for client-side navigation and hydration.

```typescript
import React from "react";
import ReactDOM from "react-dom/client";
import { RouterProvider } from "@tanstack/react-router-dom";
import { routeTree } from "./routeTree.gen";

const router = createBrowserRouter({
  routeTree,
  context: { /* your context */ },
});

ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById("root")!).render(
  <React.StrictMode>
    <RouterProvider router={router} />
  </React.StrictMode>
);

```

--------------------------------

### Enable Built-in Features

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/3.config/0.index.md

Enable specific built-in Nitro features. This example enables both 'runtimeHooks' and 'websocket' support.

```typescript
export default defineConfig({
  features: {
    runtimeHooks: true,
    websocket: true, // enable WebSocket support
  },
});
```

--------------------------------

### Implement Server Fetch Handler

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/4.examples/hello-world.md

Defines the core server entry point using the standard fetch API. This implementation is compatible with various deployment targets like Service Workers and Cloudflare Workers.

```typescript
export default {
  fetch(req: Request) {
    return new Response("Nitro Works!");
  },
};
```

--------------------------------

### POST /api/test

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/4.examples/api-routes.md

An example of a POST handler that processes JSON request bodies.

```APIDOC
## POST /api/test

### Description
Processes a JSON request body and returns a confirmation message with the received data.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/api/test

### Request Body
- **data** (object) - Optional - Any JSON payload to be echoed back.

### Request Example
{
  "key": "value"
}

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **message** (string) - Confirmation message.
- **body** (object) - The echoed request body.

### Response Example
{
  "message": "Test post handler",
  "body": {
    "key": "value"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Directory Options

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/50.configuration.md

Set custom directory paths for server, build, and output artifacts.

```ts
import { defineConfig } from "nitro";

export default defineConfig({
  serverDir: "server",
  buildDir: "node_modules/.nitro",
  output: {
    dir: ".output",
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Node.js Server Entry with Fastify

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/examples/fastify/README.md

This snippet demonstrates how to set up the server entry point for a Node.js environment using Fastify. It initializes a Fastify app, defines a basic route, and prepares the app for use by Nitro. The `.node.ts` suffix ensures this file is specific to Node.js runtimes.

```typescript
import Fastify from "fastify";

const app = Fastify();

app.get("/", () => "Hello, Fastify with Nitro!");

await app.ready();

export default app.routing;
```

--------------------------------

### Database Handler Usage

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/50.database.md

Example of using the database within a Nitro handler to create tables and perform CRUD operations.

```ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";
import { useDatabase } from "nitro/database";

export default defineHandler(async () => {
  const db = useDatabase();

  // Create users table
  await db.sql`DROP TABLE IF EXISTS users`;
  await db.sql`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users ("id" TEXT PRIMARY KEY, "firstName" TEXT, "lastName" TEXT, "email" TEXT)`;

  // Add a new user
  const userId = String(Math.round(Math.random() * 10_000));
  await db.sql`INSERT INTO users VALUES (${userId}, 'John', 'Doe', '')`;

  // Query for users
  const { rows } = await db.sql`SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${userId}`;

  return {
    rows,
  };
});
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Preview Command

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/3.config/0.index.md

Specify the command to be used for previewing the built application.

```typescript
export default defineConfig({
  commands: {
    preview: "node ./server/index.mjs",
  },
});
```

--------------------------------

### Set Production Preset

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/3.config/0.index.md

Configure the production preset for your Nitro build. This example sets the preset to 'cloudflare_pages' for deployment on Cloudflare Pages.

```typescript
export default defineConfig({
  preset: "cloudflare_pages", // deploy to Cloudflare Pages
});
```

--------------------------------

### API Route Handler Example

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/6.server-entry.md

This is a standard Nitro API route handler that defines a simple JSON response for the `/api/hello` endpoint.

```typescript
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";

export default defineHandler((event) => {
  return { hello: "API" };
});
```

--------------------------------

### Server-Side Rendering with Highlight API

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/4.examples/shiki.md

Use `<script server>` to define server-side logic. The `hl` function calls an API via `serverFetch` to highlight code. Triple-brace syntax `{{{ }}}` is crucial for rendering unescaped HTML output from the highlighted code.

```html
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
    <title>Hello World Snippet</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css" />
  </head>
  <body>
    <div class="card" role="region" aria-label="Code snippet">
      <div class="label">JavaScript</div>
      <script server>
        const hl = (code) =>
          serverFetch("/api/highlight", {
            method: "POST",
            body: code,
          });
      </script>
      <pre><code>{{{ hl(`console.log("💚 Simple is beautiful!");`) }}}</code></pre>
    </div>
  </body>
</html>

```

--------------------------------

### Define Custom Tasks for Nitro

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/3.config/0.index.md

Define custom tasks with a handler path and an optional description. This example shows a 'db:migrate' task.

```typescript
tasks: {
  'db:migrate': {
    handler: './tasks/db-migrate',
    description: 'Run database migrations'
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Development Storage Override Configuration

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/8.storage.md

Demonstrates how to use `devStorage` to override production storage configurations during development. This allows using alternative drivers like `fs` locally when production drivers are unavailable.

```typescript
import { defineConfig } from "nitro";

export default defineConfig({
  storage: {
    db: {
      driver: "redis",
      host: "prod.example.com",
    }
  },
  devStorage: {
    db: {
      driver: "fs",
      base: "./.data/db"
    }
  }
})
```

--------------------------------

### Execute tasks via API

Source: https://github.com/nitrojs/nitro/blob/main/docs/1.docs/50.tasks.md

Tasks can be triggered via GET or POST requests. Body payloads will overwrite query parameter values.

```ts
export default defineTask({
  meta: {
    name: "echo:payload",
    description: "Returns the provided payload",
  },
  run({ payload, context }) {
    console.log("Running echo task...");
    return { result: payload };
  },
});
```

```json
// [GET] /_nitro/tasks/echo:payload?field=value&array=1&array=2
{
  "field": "value",
  "array": ["1", "2"]
}
```

```json
/**
 * [POST] /_nitro/tasks/echo:payload?field=value
 * body: {
 *   "payload": {
 *     "answer": 42,
 *     "nested": {
 *       "value": true
 *     }
 *   }
 * }
 */
{
  "field": "value",
  "answer": 42,
  "nested": {
    "value": true
  }
}
```
